title: SMS / dumb-pipe positioning beat absent from architectural surfaces
content: Observed: TCP/IP positioning beat appears in two architectural surfaces (meta description and og:description). SMS / dumb-pipe positioning beat appears in zero architectural surfaces. Governance §"Canonical Positioning Beats" reads: TCP/IP "SHOULD be paired with the SMS / dumb-pipe beat to offset semantic baggage." This is a SHOULD, not a MUST; it is not enforced by the CI script's forbidden-patterns list. Not blocking; flagged for the next homepage edit pass.

title: The audit infrastructure works as designed
content: The drift the governance was built to prevent (the day-3 "two agents" framing in three architectural surfaces, captured in journal/2026-05-03-ams-homepage-n-cardinality-and-dumb-pipe.tsv) is now mechanically blocked. The script catches forbidden cardinality patterns via .github/workflows/homepage-architectural-claims.yml on every PR touching the governed paths. The five-surface matcher correctly locates all five governed surfaces in the current homepage (no exit 2 surface-missing failure). Bootstrap-day verification — running the audit on the same day governance landed and the cardinality fix shipped — is the strongest possible signal that the loop is closed.
